titles.title: ラーメン大好き小泉さん
Ms. Koizumi Loves Ramen Noodles (Ramen Daisuki Koizumi-san)

data.title: Ramen Daisuki Koizumi-san
data.title_japanese: ラーメン大好き小泉さん
data.synopsis:
From standing in the sun for hours to traveling miles away from home, gorgeous high school student Koizumi stops at nothing to fulfill her desire for ramen. But these previously solo trips soon change when Koizumi's classmate Yuu Oosawa develops an infatuation with her, and begins to join Koizumi uninvitedly on her adventures. As Yuu continues to be shocked by Koizumi's enormous appetite, she learns about the endless variety of ramen from, arguably, its greatest connoisseur ever!

[Written by MAL Rewrite]

titles.title: Magia Record: Mahou Shoujo Madoka☆Magica Gaiden PVs
マギアレコード 魔法少女まどか☆マギカ外伝
Puella Magi Madoka Magica Side Story: Magia Record

data.title: Magia Record: Mahou Shoujo Madoka☆Magica Gaiden PVs
data.title_english: Puella Magi Madoka Magica Side Story: Magia Record
data.title_japanese: マギアレコード 魔法少女まどか☆マギカ外伝
data.synopsis:
The new heroine of Magia Record is Iroha, a magical girl on the hunt for her missing sister Ui in Kamihama. Also in Kamihama is Madoka, who's searching for the similarly-missing Homura.

(Source: Crunchyroll)
data.background: Magia Record is a magical girl mobile game spin off from the Mahou Shoujo Madoka★Magica franchise. This entry contains the promotional videos created to advertise the game as they contain new and original animated footage and dialogue. The game was released on iOS and Android on August 22 2017 in Japan, and on June 25, 2019 in North America.

Meant to showcase sausages, bacon, ham, etc. that have a rustic  forest flavor with no added colors and reduced sodium. Episodes were available to view by entering a code online that was given on the inner label of a Mori no Kaori's product package.

titles.title: 紙兎ロペ TOHOシネマズ

data.title: Kamiusagi Rope x TOHO Cinemas (Movies)
data.title_japanese: 紙兎ロペ TOHOシネマズ
data.synopsis:
Kamiusagi Rope episodes for TOHO Cinemas that aired between theater commercials. Most are collaborations or advertisements and they are not considered to be a part of the movie series (ie. Kamiusagi Rope, Kamiusagi Rope 2, Kamiusagi Rope 3) due to being advertisements as well as having an irregular releases.

Some were shown at all TOHO Cinemas (eg. Kashiwa was shown everywhere to advertise the new Kashiwa TOHO Cinemas opening the following month) while others were regional restricted (eg. Hissawwaza was shown only in TOHO Cinemas Shinjuku in honor of the theater's grand opening as well as being a collaboration with the Godzilla franchise and Tokyo Metro's new IC Touch campaign).

None of these episodes were released online at a later date nor included in any DVD release of Kamiusagi Rope.

data.title: Kemono Friends 2
data.title_japanese: けものフレンズ2
data.synopsis:
Serval and Caracal find a "human" child in the forest. As they travel with the child, they meet new Friends, uncover the secrets of Japari Park, and become involved in an event that will "shake the earth."

(Source: ANN)

titles.title: Busou Shoujo Machiavellianism: Doki! "Goken-darake" no Ian Ryokou
武装少女マキャヴェリズム ドキッ！「五剣だらけ」の慰安旅行

data.title: Busou Shoujo Machiavellianism: Doki! "Goken-darake" no Ian Ryokou
data.title_japanese: 武装少女マキャヴェリズム ドキッ！「五剣だらけ」の慰安旅行
data.synopsis:
After the fierce battle with Kirukiru Amou, the school's strongest students, the Supreme Five Swords, are prepared to take the blame for the conflict. To their surprise, instead of condemning anyone, the principal abruptly assigns the group to a recreational trip to a hot spring! Moreover, they are allowed to take the infamous transfer student Fudou Nomura with them—so long as he passes his supplementary exams first. With the girls of the Swords helping him, Fudou must earn the right to unwind from his busy school life.

[Written by MAL Rewrite]
data.background: Busou Shoujo Machiavellianism: Doki! "Goken-darake" no Ian Ryokou was bundled with the seventh volume of the manga.

data.title: ChäoS;Child: Silent Sky
data.title_japanese: CHAOS;CHILD SILENT SKY
data.synopsis: Silent Sky is a combination of the additional episodes 13 and 14. It consists of the story in the future, three months after the incident that was similar to "New Generation Madness" and the disease Chaos Child Syndrome that brought by the past earthquake in Shibuya.

data.title: Mini Rope
data.title_japanese: miniロペ
data.synopsis: A short broadcast of Kamiusagi Rope meant to promote the feature film that was released during the middle as well as promote features of the Nihon Eiga Senmon Channel (Japanese Movie Specialty Channel) which is a luxury TV channel that specializes in broadcasting films that costs about 700 yen a month. Rope and Akira talk about what "mono" sound is and what does the "service" feature actually do.

titles.title: Cardcaptor Sakura: Clear Card-hen Prologue - Sakura to Futatsu no Kuma
カードキャプターさくら クリアカード編 プロローグ さくらとふたつのくま
Cardcaptor Sakura: Clear Card Prologue – Sakura and the Two Bears
Cardcaptor Sakura: Clear Clard. Episodio 23: Sakura y los dos Osos.

data.title: Cardcaptor Sakura: Clear Card-hen Prologue - Sakura to Futatsu no Kuma
data.title_english: Cardcaptor Sakura: Clear Card Prologue – Sakura and the Two Bears
data.title_japanese: カードキャプターさくら クリアカード編 プロローグ さくらとふたつのくま
data.synopsis: After the conclusion of the Sakura Card Arc, life is going back to normal. There are no more mysteries, no more disturbances of evil, no more tests of strength. Everyone is moving on with their lives and Sakura feels a sadness in her heart. Even through the goodbyes, Sakura perseveres to keep everyone and a special someone, dear to her heart.
data.background: This episode was released bundled with the special edition of the third volume of Cardcaptor Sakura: Clear Card. It adapts the ending of the original manga accurately as opposed to the original series and The Sealed Card which had an original ending. However, the TV anime of Cardcaptor Sakura: Clear Card follows the original anime's continuity rather than the manga or prologue OVA.
